commands.text: The door is locked.
The door is locked.
The door is locked.
The door is locked.
The door is locked.
The door is locked.
The door is locked.
The door is locked.
The door is locked.
The door is locked.
The door is locked.
The door is locked.

A System Hub crystal hangs in the air, humming.
Interface with the System?

else.text: SYSTEM: Catalogued. A nearby anomaly has been flagged for you, [designation]. Clear it — and be rewarded.

then.text: A System-twisted creature snarls and lunges!
The threat is down. Mira will want to know.

else.text: Something rustles in the grass — but Mira said to meet her first.

commands.text: NOTICE — By order of the Vanguard: any soul who abandons the System’s protection forfeits its aid. The Untethered are not to be sheltered.
Scratched underneath, in a shaking hand: “They took Joran for going off-grid. He only wanted to be left alone.”

then.text: Still in one piece. Good. The Reach eats the careless — and the System feeds it the rest.
You're back — and the field's quiet. The System logged the kill already, of course. It logs your heartbeat if you let it.
Here. A little field medicine. Out there you patch yourself or you don't get up — no crystal does it free.
Welcome to Dawnhearth, Awakened. Try to stay yourself out there.
That thing's still prowling the east field. Put it down — then come back and I'll see to you.

else.text: Easy, now. Eyes open. You just Awakened — the System pulled you up out of nothing, same as everyone here.
I'm Mira. I catch the new ones before the Reach does. Ask, if you've questions — most don't, until it's too late.

then.text: The System? It Awakened you. Gave you a Class, a place on its ledger. It'll heal you, arm you, carry you — for a price you can't read on any receipt.
Every favor raises your Surveillance. Lean too hard and it takes an interest. Then the Vanguard come to 'check on you.'
Oldest town in the Drowned Reach. Every Awakened opens their eyes here. We've buried more of you than I can count, and welcomed as many new ones by morning.
Don't dwell on it. Get your feet under you.
You're [name] now — the name you gave the crystal. To the System you're [designation]. A number. Try to stay more than that.

else.text: Enough talk. Touch the crystal in the plaza — let the System 'welcome' you properly. Then come back to me.

commands.text: You've got the look. They all do, the morning they come up — blinking, grateful, half-belonging to it already.
I've watched a great many first mornings, Awakened. Strange thing — I see so few of you a second time.
Eh. Old men talk. Go on.
My Edran calls the System for everything. A scraped knee, a cold supper — tap the crystal, tap the crystal.
Now the readouts say his Surveillance is 'elevated.' Two Vanguard came to 'check on him.' He sits in the dark and won't speak.
Don't lean on it like he did. Please.
You're NEW! I can tell. Mira catches the new ones — she's up by the road.
Are you gonna fight the thing in the east field?? Everyone's too scared. Bet you're not!
You're headed to the crystal, aren't you. Everyone does. My brother stopped, once.
Joran wasn't a criminal. He just stopped letting it watch him. Wanted to be left alone.
Then the Vanguard came. Now there's a locked door where his home was, and no Joran. Don't tell them I said so.
Move along, Awakened. The System sees you're freshly cataloged. Good. Stay cataloged.
And don't go asking after the Untethered. Charity to the off-grid is charity to the Calamity. We remember who asks.
An old stone well, the water clean and cold. Names are scratched into the rim — hundreds, the oldest worn smooth and carved over again.
A crisp poster in the System's cold blue: “THE SYSTEM PROVIDES. REMAIN CLASSIFIED. REPORT THE UNTETHERED. Your safety is its purpose.”
It is the only thing in Dawnhearth that never weathers.
A memorial stone ringed with fresh flowers. The names have been re-carved so often the granite is thin. None of them bear dates.
